name: discord-qdrant-horizontal-scaling-enable-cluster-mode-safety
prompt: Qdrant Version: 1.16.3. Setup: Local. I have an existing single node qdrant setup with data on it. Is it safe for my existing data to use it to create a new cluster via enabling the cluster setting? Any downtime beyond restarting? It's fine if I need to manually reshard the existing data after the fact. Just wanna double check since it's not explicitly stated afaik in the distributed deployment docs.

rubric.text: States that it is safe, existing data stays put, and the only downtime is the restart itself
Explains the node becomes peer #1 of a new single-peer cluster once cluster mode is enabled
Mentions starting the node with a --uri flag pointing to itself so future peers can discover it
Notes that new peers added later come up empty and data does not auto-move to them
States that shard_number is fixed at collection creation, so existing collections are stuck at their original shard count, and self-hosted cannot reshard them in place
Notes that automated resharding is Cloud-only
Recommends creating a new collection with the desired shard count and migrating, or replicating via new shard replicas, as the self-hosted options
Notes port 6335 (internal p2p port) should be isolated from outside access
Recommends taking a snapshot before the restart as a safety precaution
